author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
āmi narapaśuprāya, ācāravihīna tāya,
anādi ananta suvistāra
atikaṣṭe parihārya, sahajete anivārya,
aśubhera āspada ābāra

tumi ta’ dayāra sindhu, tumi ta’ jagadbandhu,
asīma vātsalya⋅payonidhi
tava guṇagaṇa smari’, bhavabandha cheda kari’,
nirbhīka hoiba niravadhi

ei icchā kari’ mane, śrī⋅yāmuna⋅caraṇe,
gāya bhaktivinoda ekhana
jāmuna⋅vipina⋅vidhu, śrī⋅caraṇāmbuja⋅sīdhu,
tā’ra śire karuna arpaṇa
en_translation:
O Hari! I am like a human animal, devoid of proper conduct, trapped in an endless, beginningless expanse of material existence. Misfortune is exceedingly difficult to avoid, yet easily becomes inevitable, and I am the abode of inauspiciousness.

But You are indeed an ocean of mercy, You are indeed the friend of the universe, an unlimited ocean of parental affection. Remembering Your many qualities and severing the bonds of material existence, I shall become fearless forever.

With this desire in my heart, at the lotus feet of Śrī Yāmuna, Bhaktivinoda now sings: O moon of the Yamunā forest, may the nectar of Your lotus feet be offered upon my head.
